items.reviewerNotes: Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Image shows a vehicle going straight; top candidate q0230's prompt = 'Let the straight-going vehicle go first' (correct=Right). Source options A=Yes/B=No → maps to A=Yes.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Clear round speed-limit sign '90' in image. q0367 correct text = 90 km/h; source option D = 90 km/h, so map master answer to source D.
Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Exact q0304 intersection image match; source statement says the current vehicle has priority, which is false in this scene, so French B=No maps to q0304 Wrong.
Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Exact q0478 intersection image match; safe action is to pass only after confirming it is safe. French D is the only option with that meaning.
Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Text matches q0224: during deferred license checking/renewal period, the holder must not drive. French A=Yes matches q0224 Right.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Image is a red-bordered circular sign '3.5' with vertical arrows = height limit. q0841 master correct = '3.5m height limit' which corresponds to source option A.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Dashboard battery icon shown. q0564's correct response = statement 'indicates generator is charging' is False; source options A=Yes/B=No → map to B=No.

Codex high-risk createNewQuestion: Valid ROW item for the q0495 penalty concept: fleeing after an accident without crime gives 12 points. Existing q0495 is MCQ, so do not reuse it for a ROW item; French A=Yes.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Source road marking is the same central-circle marking as q0803. French D=cercle central maps to the master correct answer.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Blue rectangular sign with right-turn arrow visible. q0868 master correct = 'turn right'. Source option B = 'tourner à droite' → map to B.
Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Single red slash over blue circle is no long stopping/no long parking, matching q0788. French correct row is C; previous D confused it with full no parking.

Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Source asks effect of rain; correct meaning is 'road is wet, slippery and visibility poor' which matches q0175. In this localized option set that meaning is option B.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Dashboard image and extracted answer indicate coolant level low; q0629 matches ('lack of coolant'). Localized correct option is D.

Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Question asks what to do before restarting from roadside; correct action is to observe surroundings. That meaning corresponds to q0154; in this localized option set it's option C.

Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Image clearly shows a '110' speed-limit sign. q0480 has 110km/h as the correct numeric meaning; in this localized option ordering 110 corresponds to B. Some machine matches conflict, so moderate risk.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Green ETC lane sign exactly matches q0662. French A=voie de telepeage maps to the master ETC-lane answer.

Codex high-risk approveExistingQid: Source asks forged Carte Grise/registration certificate penalty points. q0981 raw master prompt and KO/JA translations are forged vehicle transit permit with the same 12/6/3/2 options; normalized English prompt appears drifted. French A=12.

Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Scenario image and prompt ask who to yield to when going straight; correct behavior is to yield to vehicle from the right. q0503 expresses 'yield to vehicle from right' — localized option B matches.

Codex high-risk createNewQuestion: Valid ROW version of the coolant-low dashboard symbol concept. q0629 is an MCQ asking what the indicator means, not the same ROW statement, so create new; French A=Yes.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Statement 'following vehicle may use high beam at night' is false; q0051 matches this rule (correctRow = Wrong). In the localized Yes/No options, 'No' is option B.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Image shows a highway entrance; top candidate q0260 and other image-based candidates indicate the manoeuvre is incorrect (Wrong). Source options map 'No' to B.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Guide arrow marking means right turn ahead and matches q0706 image. French A=tourner a droite maps to master correct D.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Question asks licence ban after fleeing an accident constituting a crime; q0496's correct answer is 'for lifetime' which matches source option C ('Toute la vie').

Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Dashboard shows red battery symbol (+/-). Visual semantics and top candidate q0584 indicate charging/charging-circuit malfunction; that corresponds to source option D. There is a conflicting OCR/extracted key (B), so moderate risk.

Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Statement '50% over speed -> 3 points' is matched by q0280 which marks that claim as Wrong. Candidates support 'Wrong'. Legal specifics could vary; moderate confidence.

Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Sign shows horn in red prohibition circle — meaning 'no honking'. q0889 has 'no honking' as the correct meaning; source option C is 'défense de klaxonner'. Top machine candidate (q0657) had conflicting mapping, so flagged moderate risk. Rejected top matcher: Top matcher q0657's suggested correct option conflicts with the visual meaning ('should honk' vs actual prohibition); candidates disagree.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Question about seat belt function; q0626's correct answer is 'to reduce injuries of driver and passengers', matching source option A.

Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Image clearly shows a '120' speed sign. q0399 has correctOptionText 120 and matches the visual. Top machine candidate q0367 suggested different value (90), so flagged moderate risk due to candidate conflict. Rejected top matcher: Top matcher q0367 predicts 90km/h which conflicts with the visible '120' sign and with q0399 image candidate.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Statement 'prohibited to overtake on narrow road/bridge' matches q0074 which is True/Right. Candidates support this meaning.
Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Source states unpaid fines cause penalty points to carry into next cycle; q0233 explicitly matches that behavior. Candidate ranking lower but semantics align.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Source road sign is a direction sign and exactly matches q0771. French D=signal de direction maps to the master answer.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Blue rectangular lane sign means straight-going lane and matches q0878. French A=aller tout droit maps to master correct D.
Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Source marking image matches q0220. The statement that it means accelerate is false; French B=No maps to q0220 Wrong.
Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Yellow warning/slow sign matches q0813. French C=circuler a basse vitesse is the localized answer for passing slowly.

Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: q0063 is the exact fuller night-driving statement including shorter visibility, poorer observation, and fatigue from concentration; q0101 only covers the first half. French A=Yes.
Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Source negative statement about not needing front/side/rear lights in dusty weather matches q0285, which is false. French B=No.

Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Source roadside parking image and statement match q0193: this parking behavior is illegal/unsafe, so the statement that no illegal act occurred is false. French B=No.

Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Dashboard icon shows yellow car with open hood — semantics match q0548 'indicates hood open'. Approve mapping; machine candidate answer inconsistent but visual semantics are clear.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Statement about tire blowout — do not brake abruptly; shift to low gear and use engine braking — matches q0088 (Right/Yes).

Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Question about narrow mountain road: correct action is 'reduce speed or stop and yield' which corresponds to Q0144 semantics. Machine candidate q0144 present; map to local option B (reduce speed/stop).

Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Yellow triangular warning sign with worker pictogram = roadworks. Top candidate q0888 has identical options and correct = construction zone → source option B ('tronson en travaux'). Strong image match.

Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Text asks which action leads to 200–2000 yuan fine and license revocation. Top candidate q0405 matches wording exactly; master correct = driving 50% over limit → source option B.

Codex high-risk approveExistingQid: Source says headrest protects the head in collision. q0531 raw/prior translations use the same headrest/head wording and mark it false; normalized master wording has drift. French B=No.
Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Source asks where overtaking is prohibited; q0455 exact concept, and French B=intersections maps to the master correct answer.

Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Prompt asks punishment for driving an out-of-service (write-off) vehicle. Top candidate q0404 correct = revoke license (master D); maps to source option B ('retrait du permis de conduire').

Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Statement says police may legally detain crashed vehicle to collect evidence. Top candidate q0212 matches prompt and marks statement True → source option A (Yes).

Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Asks where to change lane before intersection. Top candidate q0111 correct = area marked by broken lines per guide arrow → source option C corresponds to that wording.
Codex low-risk approveExistingQid: Statement about ABS preserving steering during hard braking. Top candidate q0574 matches and marks statement True → source option A (Yes).

Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Source claims police do NOT have right to question drivers who remained but should have left; top candidate q0250 gives similar statement and labels it Wrong. That maps to source option B (No). Slight wording differences lower confidence.

Codex medium-risk approveExistingQid: Asks main influence of mountain roads; top candidate q0180 correct = long slopes, sharp curves, shorter visibility (master B) which corresponds to source option C. Good semantic match though scores modest.
